web.acp.bootHint: Changes are applied immediately. Delegated agents still require approval before they start, and env values can use {{ref}} references.
web.acp.osSandboxHint: Off by default. This only takes effect when the daemon sandbox is enabled. When active, the adapter is confined to session roots while Codex and Claude login directories stay available for authentication.
web.acp.empty: No external agents yet. Add Codex or Claude Code to delegate subtasks from monad.
web.acp.forwardMcpHint: Off by default. When enabled, monad forwards configured MCP servers and resolved secrets to this agent. Only enable this for agents you trust.
web.acp.inviteHint: Choose a preset, review the command, then register the agent for delegation.
web.acp.name: Name
web.acp.presetHint.claude-code: Install Claude Code (npm i -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code) and run `claude` to log in, or set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.
web.acp.presetHint.codex: Install Codex (the Codex.app or npm i -g @openai/codex) and run `codex login`.
web.acp.authGuidance.claudeIntro: Claude Code needs Anthropic API credentials. Either of these works:
web.acp.authGuidance.codexIntro: Codex needs OpenAI API credentials. Either of these works:
web.acp.authGuidance.envRef: 1. Add `{{key}}` to the ACP agent config's env with value `{{ref}}` (make sure the daemon environment has that variable)
web.acp.authGuidance.claudeLogin: 2. Run `claude login` in a terminal to refresh the OAuth token
web.acp.authGuidance.codexLogin: 2. Run `codex login` in a terminal to refresh the login state
web.acp.authGuidance.generic: This ACP agent needs valid API credentials. Add the matching API key to the agent config's env, or check the adapter's login state.
web.acp.subtitle: Agents monad can delegate subtasks to
web.acp.title: External agents
web.nativeCli.bootHint: Native CLI agents run as provider-owned clients. Monad launches and relays them, but provider tools, skills, MCP, approvals, and session state stay owned by each CLI provider.
web.nativeCli.connectHint: Use this provider-owned terminal to finish login, folder trust, or first-run setup. Monad only relays terminal input and output.
web.nativeCli.connectTerminalHint: Click the terminal and use the CLI directly.
web.nativeCli.dangerousModeHint: Off by default. Dangerous provider flags are rejected unless this is explicitly enabled for the agent.
web.nativeCli.empty: No native CLI agents yet. Connect a detected native CLI to invite its full client into a project.
web.nativeCli.inviteHint: Choose a provider preset, review the command, then register it for project invites.
web.nativeCli.presetHint.claude-code: Install Claude Code and run `claude` to log in.
web.nativeCli.presetHint.gemini: Install Gemini CLI and run `gemini` to log in.
web.nativeCli.subtitle: Provider-owned native clients
web.nativeCli.title: Native CLI agents
web.approvals.desc: Tools you allowed or denied without asking again. Revoke an entry to review it next time.
web.atoms.addHint: Installing runs the atoms declared by this pack. Only install sources you trust.
web.atoms.conflicts: Name conflicts. Pin a winner or remove a pack.
web.atoms.consentTitle: This atom pack declares the following atoms:
web.atoms.subtitle: Install and manage atom packs for tools, connectors, commands, and providers.
web.atoms.title: Atoms
web.ch.addHint: After you create the channel, add its bot token and authorized users. New channels deny all users until a token and access policy are configured.
web.ch.empty: No channels yet. Add one to connect an IM platform.
web.ch.label: Label
web.ch.subtitle: Connect IM platforms to the agent.
web.ch.title: Channels
web.chat.compactNoop: Nothing to compact yet — the recent context is already kept verbatim.
web.chat.placeholder: Message monad... Use / for skills. Shift+Enter adds a line break.
web.chat.readOnly: Read-only. Send messages from the originating client.
web.chat.restoreConfirm: Rewind to this message? Later messages will be deleted.
web.inspector.emptyHint: Send a message to see the loop assemble context, call the model, stream output, and run tools.
web.inspector.selectNodeHint: Click any loop card to inspect its projected UI item, status, sequence, and raw payload.
web.inspector.sessionRuntime: Runtime activity is inferred from this session's live UI stream.
web.inspector.subtitle: Inspectable runtime view from messages, context, approvals, tools, and extension events.
web.inspector.title: Agent loop inspector
web.conn.certExpiryHint: Run monad tls renew on the daemon, then restart it to issue a new certificate.
web.conn.certFpChangedHint: The daemon TLS certificate is different from the one you trusted before. Verify it with the daemon operator, then trust the new fingerprint if it is expected.
web.conn.certFpNew: First use — fingerprint pinned. You'll be warned if it changes.
web.conn.intro: monad connects to the local daemon by default. To control monad on another machine, enter its remote address after remote access is enabled there.
web.conn.allowInsecureHttpDesc: Only use this behind a TLS-terminating proxy. Otherwise the bearer token is sent in cleartext.
web.conn.localRemoteDesc: Bind the local daemon on the network and protect remote requests with a bearer token.
web.conn.restartRequired: Restart the daemon for bind address and TLS changes to take effect.
web.conn.title: Connection
web.conn.tlsWarningCertError: TLS certificate generation failed. Check the daemon log. Until this is fixed, the bearer token is sent without encryption.
web.conn.tlsWarningOpenssl: OpenSSL is not installed on the server. Install it to enable automatic HTTPS and protect the bearer token in transit.
web.conn.tlsWarningTitle: Remote access is running over plain HTTP
web.conn.tokenPlaceholder: secret token from the remote daemon's config
web.conn.urlHint: Leave blank to connect to the local daemon via /api.
web.init.agentError: We could not create the agent. Check the fields and try again.
web.init.connFailed: Connection failed. Check the provider settings and try again.
web.init.connFailedKey: Connection failed. Check the API key and try again.
web.init.descAddKey: Enter credentials, then test the connection before saving.
web.init.descAgent: Name the agent and choose its default model.
web.init.descHome: Choose where monad stores local data. Leave this blank to use the default.
web.init.descList: Add at least one provider with an API key to start using monad.
web.init.descModel: Choose which model monad uses by default.
web.init.doneDesc: monad is configured and ready to use.
web.init.homeError: We could not set that home directory. Check the path and try again.
web.init.homeRestartNote: The daemon will restart to use the new path.
web.init.modelsFound_one: {{count}} model found. Pick a default in the next step.
web.init.modelsFound_other: {{count}} models found. Pick a default in the next step.
web.init.restarting: Restarting daemon with new home directory…
web.init.saveError: We could not save settings. Try again.
web.mcp.authOauthNote: OAuth is configured in config.json and left unchanged here.
web.mcp.bootHint: Changes are applied immediately. The daemon reconnects servers after save, and tools still pass through the approval gate. Secret values can use {{ref}} references.
web.mcp.empty: No MCP servers yet. Add a stdio subprocess or HTTP endpoint.
web.mcp.name: Name
web.mcp.atomPackServersHint: Installed from a registry, command, or binary. Connect live — no restart.
web.mcp.configuredServersHint: Declared in config.json. Apply on the next daemon restart.
web.mcp.subtitle: External tool servers the agent can connect to.
web.mcp.title: MCP servers
web.mcpAtom.browseEmpty: No MCP servers match your search.
web.mcpAtom.browseError: Search failed. Check your connection and try again.
web.mcpAtom.subtitle: Install MCP servers from npx, uvx, a URL, or a prebuilt binary.
web.mcpAtom.title: MCP atoms
web.model.fastModelHint: Used for lightweight operations: skill forks, context summaries, and quick background tasks. Falls back to the default model when not set.
web.model.embedSwitchBody: Existing message vectors were built with the previous model and will not match the new one. Rebuild the index, or keep the existing vectors unchanged.
web.model.keyValidatedNote: The key is validated by listing the provider’s models — only added on success.
web.model.labelKeyRequired: Enter both a label and key.
web.model.needBaseUrl: This provider type needs a Base URL.
web.model.noProviders: No providers yet — add one to get started.
web.model.profileHelp: Use a short alias, then choose the provider and model it resolves to.
web.model.profilesDesc: Profiles are sets of model settings that let agents choose the right model for each runtime scenario.
web.model.providersDesc: Add model providers and validate credentials before Monad uses them.
web.model.roleNoCandidates: No matching models from connected providers
web.model.rolesHint: Pick a model for each capability. Candidates are filtered to connected providers' models that support the role. Unset roles fall back to the default model.
web.model.subtitle: Connect providers, choose default profiles, and route specialized tasks to the right model.
web.model.title: Models
web.model.usageResetConfirm: Reset the usage ledger? This clears all recorded tokens and cost.
web.settings.languageDesc: Display language for the app and channel replies.
web.settings.title: Settings
web.settings.summary: Manage runtime, tools, models, transport, and memory.
web.sidebar.indexing: Indexing {{count}} more messages. Semantic results may be incomplete.
web.sidebar.monadAgentHint: Direct chat with the host agent.
web.studio.capabilitiesMcpSubtitle: External tool servers. User-configured servers require a daemon restart; atom-pack servers connect live.
web.studio.capabilitiesToolsSubtitle: Built-in tools available to agents. Core tools are always on; optional tools can be toggled.
web.studio.descriptionHint: When should other agents delegate to this one?
web.studio.emptyBody: Agents bundle a system prompt, model, tools, and sandbox into reusable roles.
web.studio.exposeSubset: Exposure ⊆ registration: an agent can only use capabilities already registered system-wide.
web.studio.exposeWarning: Delegated and public callers inherit this agent tool access, including file-write and command-execution tools. Review atoms and sandbox settings before exposing it.
web.studio.importHint: Paste a Claude Code subagent file with YAML frontmatter. monad imports its name, description, model, and system prompt. Configure capabilities afterward.
web.studio.name: Name
web.studio.promptHint:
# System prompt

Describe how this agent should behave…
web.studio.sandboxHelp: Per-agent override of the filesystem boundary. The global sandbox still caps this.
web.studio.atomsModeDesc: Off: inherit every system-enabled atom and MCP server. On: this agent only sees what you select below.
web.studio.atomsEmpty: No atom packs or MCP servers installed yet. Add them under Capabilities.
web.studio.summary: Agents, orchestration, capabilities, and runtime behavior.
web.studio.title: Studio
web.studio.visPublicDesc: A public identity on the network others can reach.
web.studio.visSubagentDesc: Other agents can delegate to it as an isolated in-process peer.
web.studio.orchestrationDesc: Any agent can delegate a self-contained subtask to these callable subagents through agent_delegate_to. Each runs with its own persona and allowlisted tools.
web.studio.orchestrationEmpty: No agents are exposed as subagents yet.
web.studio.orchestrationEmptyHint: Turn on Callable as subagent in an agent Visibility tab to make it available for delegation.
web.studio.sandboxAllowedDomainsHint: One domain per line. Subdomains are included. Leave empty to block all egress.
web.studio.sandboxConfineHint: Kernel-level write fence for spawned code and shell processes.
web.studio.sandboxDesc: System-level confinement applied to every agent unless a narrower per-agent setting is used.
web.studio.sandboxGlobalEnabledHint: When on, every agent is forced to this mode; per-agent overrides are ignored.
web.studio.sandboxHostExecHint: Whether code_execute may run unconfined on the real machine.
web.studio.sandboxRestartHint: Confinement, network, and launcher changes take effect after the next daemon restart.
web.workspace.title: Workspace
web.workspace.summary: Start from the host Monad Agent or jump into a multi-agent channel. Studio now owns agent capabilities and runtime behavior; Settings stays system-wide.
web.workspace.agentSummary: Your fixed entry point for talking directly to the host agent.
web.workspace.localDaemonSummary: State stays on this machine by default, with remote access controlled explicitly.
web.workplace.projectSummary: Open this multi-agent project in the workplace.
web.workplace.projectPanelSummary: This project stays inside the workspace shell while preserving the multi-agent chat flow.
web.workplace.newProjectDescription: Name your project and choose a shared working folder its agents will run in.
web.workplace.noProjectsHint: Create a project to start a multi-agent workplace.
web.workplace.setFolderHint: Set the shared working folder for this project
web.workplace.membersDescription: Add or remove agents for this project, and tune their project-local options. Global enablement lives in Studio.
web.workplace.removeCliMember: Remove CLI member from this project
web.workplace.removeAcpMember: Remove ACP member from this project
web.workplace.noMembersHint: Add Codex, Claude Code, or ACP agents below. Configure global agent availability in Studio.
web.workplace.noAvailableMembers: No configured agents are available to add. Add or enable agents in Studio first.
web.workplace.enableAgentFirst: Enable this agent in Studio first
web.workspace.settingsSummary: Connection, language, providers, approvals, and system preferences.
web.workspace.studioSummary: Shape agents, capabilities, runtime policy, and orchestration.
web.skills.addHint: Install only sources you trust. Installed skills can be invoked explicitly even when global context loading is off.
web.skills.createSkillHelp: Read more about creating skills or see an example.
web.skills.fileRequirementArchive: .zip or .skill file that includes a SKILL.md file at the root level
web.skills.fileRequirementFrontmatter: SKILL.md contains a skill name and description formatted in YAML
web.skills.githubHint: Import a skill directly from a public GitHub repository.
web.skills.githubInvalidUrl: Enter a valid GitHub repository URL.
web.skills.uploadDrop: Drag and drop or click to upload
web.skills.autoloadHelpBody: When this is on, enabled skills are automatically made available in the model context.
web.skills.autoloadHelpOff: Turning it off does not uninstall or disable skills. It only stops automatic injection; you can still invoke enabled skills explicitly from chat.
web.skills.autoloadHelpPerSkill: Per-skill context switches only apply while this is on.
web.skills.autoloadHint: Let enabled skills enter the model context automatically.
web.skills.consentTitle: This repo declares the following skills:
web.skills.emptyHint: Install trusted skills to add reusable instructions and workflows to monad.
web.skills.editorHint: Edit the full SKILL.md content. Frontmatter is validated before saving.
web.skills.previewHint: Preview this skill without editing its SKILL.md content.
web.skills.installReviewHelpBody: When this is on, Monad uses the default model to review the full skill before install.
web.skills.installReviewHelpConfirm: Risky verdicts, review failures, or unavailable review results show a confirmation popover beside the install button.
web.skills.installReviewHelpUnavailable: This check depends on a usable default model; it cannot be enabled until one is configured.
web.skills.installReviewHint: Review skill content before install and confirm risky results.
web.skills.installReviewUnavailableHint: Configure a default model to enable pre-install review.
web.skills.parseMissingFields: SKILL.md frontmatter must include name and description.
web.skills.subtitle: Manage installed skills, context loading, and trusted installs.
web.skills.title: Skills
web.skills.browseError: Search failed. Check your connection and try again.
web.url.httpOnly: Enter a valid URL that starts with http:// or https://.
web.settings.import.replaceDesc: Conflicts stay blocked unless you enable updates and preview again; credentials are never overwritten automatically.
web.settings.import.noPreview: Preview an explicit local file or directory before applying anything.
web.mem0.inactive: mem0 isn't the active memory backend
web.mem0.inactiveHint: Switch the backend to mem0 in Memory settings to store and explore vector memories.
web.graph.emptyHint: Run /consolidate-graph in a chat to build it from your conversations.
web.settings.moDesc: Mo is a desktop sprite you drop files onto to start a session. Launch or stop it, and preview its animation states.
web.settings.system.upgradeHint: A newer version is available. To upgrade, run:
web.settings.system.developerModeDesc: Append session, channel, LLM, ACP, and generation communication logs to the monad home logs directory.
web.settings.system.interactiveCursorDes: Use a pointer cursor for interactive elements.
web.settings.system.resetDesc: Reset specific parts of monad. More granular reset commands are available in the CLI.
web.settings.system.resetUsageDesc: Clear cumulative token and cost counters.
web.settings.system.clearSessionsDesc: Permanently delete all {{count}} sessions and their history.
web.settings.system.cliOnlyHint: Reset session history, config, and credentials from the CLI: monad reset sessions / config / auth / all
web.licenses.title: Licenses
web.licenses.desc: Open-source licenses for third-party packages bundled with monad.
web.hooks.subtitle: Shell commands that run during session lifecycle events.
web.hooks.failClosedTitle: Fail closed. Block the step if this hook errors or times out.
web.hooks.invalidRegex: This regular expression is not valid, so it will not match anything. {{error}}
web.hooks.desc.BeforeTurn: Runs when the user submits a prompt — can mutate the prompt or override the model
web.hooks.desc.BeforeModel: Runs before each reasoning model call — can rewrite the request or short-circuit it
web.hooks.desc.BeforeTool: Runs before a tool call — can deny or mutate tool arguments
web.hooks.desc.ApprovalRequest: Runs when a tool call hits the approval gate — can allow, deny, or ask
web.hooks.desc.AfterTool: Runs after a tool call (success or failure) — can mutate the tool result
web.hooks.desc.AfterModel: Runs after a reasoning model response (success or failure) — can rewrite it
web.hooks.desc.BeforeCompact: Runs before the context is compacted — can inject what to preserve in the summary
web.hooks.desc.AfterCompact: Runs after the context is compacted
web.hooks.desc.BeforeSubagent: Runs before a forked subagent runs — can inject context
web.hooks.desc.AfterSubagent: Runs when a forked subagent finishes (success or failure) — can rewrite its result
web.hooks.desc.AfterTurn: Runs when a turn ends (completed, aborted, or errored) — can rewrite the answer or continue
web.memory.backendDesc: Choose where learned facts are stored. Built-in memory stays in local Markdown you control. mem0 delegates extraction and recall to its own models.
web.memory.qdrantIdle: Qdrant will start on the first conversation.
web.memory.factsDesc: Objective facts the agent has learned. Scope controls whether a fact belongs to all sessions, one agent, or one session.
web.memory.factPlaceholder: Add a fact, for example: User deploys with Bun, not Node.
web.memory.noFacts: No facts recorded for this scope yet.
web.memory.noFactsHint: The agent can add facts automatically, or you can add one above.
web.memory.enterScopeAgent: Enter an agent id to view its memory.
web.memory.enterScopeSession: Enter a session id to view its memory.
web.memory.mem0Automatic: mem0 extracts and recalls facts automatically on each turn. The manual memory tool and raw MEMORY.md editor do not apply.
web.memory.mem0Profiles: mem0 picks its models from your Monad profiles (Settings → Model). Leave a field on its default to follow the chat default and the embedding role.
web.memory.qdrantLocal: Memories persist to a local qdrant, downloaded automatically on first use. To use your own store instead, set
web.memory.graphDesc: The L2 knowledge graph links entities extracted from your conversations. Build it on demand with /consolidate-graph, or have the daemon keep it fresh in the background.
web.memory.graphAutoDesc: Periodically run consolidation in the background. Each run costs one extraction model call per session with new conversation.
web.tools.title: Built-in tools
web.tools.subtitle: Configure code execution, web search, and email backends.
web.tools.webSearchDesc: Fetch pages and search the web. The agent uses this to look up real-time information.
web.tools.codeExecDesc: Run code snippets in a sandboxed environment with an isolated filesystem.
web.tools.emailDesc: Send emails from the agent using SMTP, Resend, or auto-detected credentials.
web.tools.filesystemDesc: Read, write, edit, glob, and grep files inside the agent's sandbox.
web.tools.shellDesc: Execute shell commands. Every call requires your approval before it runs.
web.tools.processDesc: Start and manage long-running background processes across turns.
web.tools.networkDesc: Fetch URLs with SSRF and DNS-rebind protection; GET and HEAD only.
web.tools.webExtractDesc: Download a page and extract its readable content as clean text.
web.tools.todoDesc: In-session to-do list that structures the agent's work plan.
web.tools.memoryDesc: Session scratchpad — facts written here are recalled automatically in every later turn.
web.tools.scheduleDesc: Schedule one-shot or recurring prompts with a cron expression.
web.tools.wsAuto: Uses Brave when an API key is set. Otherwise, uses DuckDuckGo.
web.tools.wsNative: Forces provider-native search when the active model supports it; otherwise falls back to DuckDuckGo.
web.tools.wsDdgs: Uses DuckDuckGo. Free, no API key required.
web.tools.wsBrave: Uses the Brave Search API. Requires an API key.
web.tools.followSystemDesc: Delegates to whatever OS sandbox launcher is active (Seatbelt, Docker, or E2B as configured in Sandbox settings).
web.tools.dockerDesc: Runs snippets in a disposable container with isolated network, read-only filesystem, and no host credentials.
web.tools.localDesc: Runs snippets in a subprocess confined by the OS sandbox, such as Seatbelt on macOS.
web.tools.dockerInstallHint: Install Docker or Podman to enable this backend.
web.tools.dockerNotRunning: Docker or Podman is not running. Start Docker Desktop or run {{cmd}}, then restart the daemon.
web.tools.e2bDesc: Runs snippets in an E2B cloud microVM. Strongly isolated — no host access, no credentials. Requires an API key.
web.tools.e2bNotAvailable: No API key configured. Add one to enable this backend.
web.tools.browserPresetDesc: Web automation via Playwright MCP. Handles JS-rendered pages and interactive flows.
web.tools.computerPresetDesc: Desktop control MCP — screenshot, mouse, keyboard. Drives the real host desktop; mutating actions require explicit approval.
web.tools.obscuraDesc: Rust-based headless browser with 12 built-in MCP tools. Binary is downloaded from GitHub on first enable.
web.obscura.title: Obscura
web.obscura.subtitle: Headless browser MCP for JavaScript-rendered and interactive pages.
web.obscura.disableHint: After disabling, these tools will be removed from the toolset the next time the daemon restarts.
web.obscura.installNeeded: Binary not found. Enable Obscura to download it from GitHub Releases (about 30 MB).
web.obscura.installed: Binary installed. Enable Obscura to start the MCP server now.
web.api.title: API
web.api.subtitle: OpenAI-compatible endpoint
web.api.bearerHint: Leave blank to allow unauthenticated access (only safe on loopback).
web.studio.usageLotr: You've used ~{{count}}× more tokens than The Lord of the Rings.
web.oauth.failed: Authorization failed. You can close this tab.
web.studio.workshopPreviewHint: A quick read on what this agent will actually expose and inherit.
web.studio.workshopToolsHint: Atom packs and MCP servers this agent may see.
web.studio.workshopSafetyHint: Sandbox and budget caps that keep the agent contained.
web.studio.workshopVisibilityHint: How this agent gets called by others.
web.studio.workshopPromptEmpty: Drop in a prompt core or keep the agent terse.
web.studio.workshopAllowlistEnabled: Allowlist active with {{count}} mounted capability items.
web.studio.workshopAllowlistDisabled: Inherited access to every system-enabled atom and MCP server.
web.studio.workshopInspectorHint: Drag parts onto the bench, then fine-tune them here.
